essence: Keeps buses safe and road‑ready through skilled mechanical work, diagnostics, and preventative maintenance.

identity.title: Bus Mechanic / Vehicle Technician
identity.summary: Inspects, repairs, and maintains buses and large vehicles, ensuring they operate safely, efficiently, and reliably for public transport services.

salary.notes: Electric/hybrid bus specialists, diagnostic technicians, and supervisory roles can exceed £45k–£55k+.

qualifications: Level 2/3 Diploma in Heavy Vehicle Maintenance or equivalent
Apprenticeship in vehicle mechanics or engineering
Knowledge of diesel, hybrid, or electric drivetrains
Understanding of safety, diagnostics, and workshop procedures

lifestyle.description: Bus mechanics work in depots and workshops, maintaining fleets of buses to ensure safe and reliable public transport. Days involve diagnosing faults, repairing engines and electrical systems, performing routine servicing, inspecting brakes and suspension, and responding to breakdowns. The role is hands‑on, technical, and safety‑critical, often requiring early starts or shift patterns. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy mechanical problem‑solving, practical work, and keeping essential transport systems running.

habits: Uses diagnostic tools to identify faults
Stays updated on new vehicle technologies
